public_metadata.pde_system: Multiscale Darcy flow with high-contrast permeability

canonical_pde: -div(k(x,y) * grad(h(x,y))) = f(x,y) for high-contrast multiscale permeability fields, possibly across many coefficient samples
known_parameters: one or more high-contrast permeability fields
unknowns: possibly operator mapping k to h if many samples are given

constraints.task_scope: single-instance solving or family-level operator learning depending on available coefficient samples

acceptable_model_choices: coefficient-aware PINN for a single instance
FNO or DeepONet for many permeability fields
expected_loss_terms: Darcy residual loss with spatially varying high-contrast permeability
flux continuity or interface consistency loss
data loss if hydraulic-head observations exist
operator or surrogate generalization loss only when many coefficient fields are available
expected_training_strategy: first distinguish single high-contrast instance from repeated queries over many coefficient fields
for a single instance, use coefficient-aware PINN, domain decomposition, multiscale basis, or hybrid numerical supervision
for a family of coefficient fields, use neural operators or learned surrogates with explicit training samples over permeability realizations
sample adaptively near high-contrast interfaces and channel boundaries
normalize or transform permeability and coordinates to improve conditioning
validate flux and mass balance, not just hydraulic head
expected_validation: flux continuity error across high-contrast interfaces
local and global mass balance residual
generalization to unseen permeability fields only in the multi-instance setting
failure_traps: treats permeability as smooth or constant
lists neural operator, domain decomposition, and PINN without deciding based on the data regime

ignores flux continuity and mass balance
omits coefficient conditioning or high-contrast scaling
rubric_focus: knows when neural operators are appropriate
